Targeted EIDL Advance, , , provided that:Pub. L. 117–2, title V, § 5002135 Stat. 85Mar. 11, 2021“(a).—DefinitionsIn this section—“(1)the term ‘Administrator’ means the Administrator of the Small Business Administration; and“(2)the terms ‘covered entity’ and ‘economic loss’ have the meanings given the terms in section 331(a) of the Economic Aid to Hard-Hit Small Businesses, Nonprofits, and Venues Act (title III of division N of ) [].Public Law 116–26015 U.S.C. 9009b(a)“(b).—AppropriationsIn addition to amounts otherwise available, there is appropriated to the Administrator for fiscal year 2021, out of any money in the Treasury not otherwise appropriated, $15,000,000,000—“(1)to remain available until expended; and“(2)of which, the Administrator shall use—“(A)$10,000,000,000 to make payments to covered entities that have not received the full amounts to which the covered entities are entitled under section 331 of the Economic Aid to Hard-Hit Small Businesses, Nonprofits, and Venues Act (title III of division N of ) []; andPublic Law 116–26015 U.S.C. 9009b“(B)$5,000,000,000 to make payments under section 1110(e) of the CARES Act (), each of which shall be—15 U.S.C. 9009(e)“(i)made to a covered entity that—“(I)has suffered an economic loss of greater than 50 percent; and“(II)employs not more than 10 employees;“(ii)in an amount that is $5,000; and“(iii)with respect to the covered entity to which the payment is made, in addition to any payment made to the covered entity under section 1110(e) of the CARES Act () or section 331 of the Economic Aid to Hard-Hit Small Businesses, Nonprofits, and Venues Act (title III of division N of ).”15 U.S.C. 9009(e)Public Law 116–260

N, title III, § 333(a)134 Stat. 2046Dec. 27, 2020“(a).—DefinitionsIn this section [amending this section and enacting provisions set out as notes under this section]—“(1)the term ‘covered entity’ means an entity that receives an advance under section 1110(e) of the CARES Act (), including an entity that received such an advance before the date of enactment of this Act []; and15 U.S.C. 9009(e)Dec. 27, 2020“(2)the term ‘covered period’ has the meaning given the term in section 1110(a)(1) of the CARES Act (), as amended by section 332 of this Act [div. N of ].15 U.S.C. 9009(a)(1)Pub. L. 116–260“(e)Rulemaking.—“(1).—In generalNot later than 15 days after the date of enactment of this Act [], the Administrator [of the Small Business Administration] shall issue rules that ensure the equal treatment of all covered entities with respect to the amendment made by subsection (c) [amending this section], which shall include consideration of covered entities that, before the date of enactment of this Act, completed the loan forgiveness process described in section 1110(e)(6) of the CARES Act (), as in effect before that date of enactment.Dec. 27, 202015 U.S.C. 9009(e)(6)“(2).—Notice and commentThe notice and comment requirements under , United States Code, shall not apply with respect to the rules issued under paragraph (1).”section 553 of title 5

children.text: the term “covered period” means the period beginning on and ending on ; andJanuary 31, 2020December 31, 2021

children.label: A
children.text: a business with not more than 500 employees;

children.label: B
children.text: any individual who operates under a sole proprietorship, with or without employees, or as an independent contractor;

children.label: C
children.text: a cooperative with not more than 500 employees;

children.label: D
children.text: an ESOP (as defined in ) with not more than 500 employees;section 632 of this title

children.label: E
children.text: a tribal small business concern, as described in , with not more than 500 employees; orsection 657a(b)(2)(C) of this title

children.label: F
children.text: an agricultural enterprise (as defined in with not more than 500 employees.section 647(b) of this title1So in original. Probably should be followed by a closing parenthesis.1

children.heading: Eligible entities
children.label: b Eligible entities
children.text: During the covered period, in addition to small business concerns, private nonprofit organizations, and small agricultural cooperatives, an eligible entity shall be eligible for a loan made under .section 636(b)(2) of this title
any rules related the personal guarantee on advances and loans of not more than $200,000 during the covered period for all applicants;2So in original. Probably should be followed by “to”.2

the requirement that an applicant needs to be in business for the 1-year period before the disaster, except that no waiver may be made for a business that was not in operation on ; andJanuary 31, 2020

the requirement in the flush matter following subparagraph (E) of , as so redesignated by subsection (f) of this section, that an applicant be unable to obtain credit elsewhere.section 636(b)(2) of this title3See Codification note below.3

children.heading: Approval and ability to repay for small dollar loans
children.label: d Approval and ability to repay for small dollar loans
A
children.text: based solely on the credit score of the applicant; or

children.label: B
children.text: by using alternative appropriate methods to determine an applicant’s ability to repay; and

children.label: A
children.text: an applicant is eligible to receive such a loan; or

children.label: B
children.text: the information contained in an application for such a loan is accurate.

children.heading: Advances
children.label: A Advances
children.text: During the covered period, an entity included for eligibility in subsection (b), including small business concerns, private nonprofit organizations, and small agricultural cooperatives, that applies for a loan under in response to COVID–19 may request that the Administrator provide an advance that is, subject to paragraph (3), in the amount requested by such applicant to such applicant.section 636(b)(2) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: verify whether the entity is an entity that is eligible for a loan made under during the covered period, as described in subsection (b);section 636(b)(2) of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: if the Administrator, under clause (i), verifies that the entity submitting the request is an entity that is eligible, as described in that clause, provide the advance requested by the entity; and

children.label: iii
children.text: with respect to an entity that the Administrator determines is not entitled to receive an advance under this subsection, provide the entity with a notification explaining why the Administrator reached that determination.

children.heading: Verification
children.label: 2 Verification
children.text: Before disbursing amounts under this subsection, the Administrator shall verify that the applicant is an eligible entity by accepting a self-certification from the applicant under penalty of perjury pursuant to .section 1746 of title 28

children.heading: Amount
children.label: 3 Amount
children.text: The amount of an advance provided under this subsection shall be not more than $10,000.

children.label: A
children.text: providing paid sick leave to employees unable to work due to the direct effect of the COVID–19;

children.label: B
children.text: maintaining payroll to retain employees during business disruptions or substantial slowdowns;

children.label: C
children.text: meeting increased costs to obtain materials unavailable from the applicant’s original source due to interrupted supply chains;

children.label: D
children.text: making rent or mortgage payments; and

children.label: E
children.text: repaying obligations that cannot be met due to revenue losses.

children.heading: Repayment
children.label: 5 Repayment
children.text: An applicant shall not be required to repay any amounts of an advance provided under this subsection, even if subsequently denied a loan under .section 636(b)(2) of this title

children.heading: Repealed. , ,Pub. L. 116–260, div. N, title III, § 333(c)134 Stat. 2046Dec. 27, 2020
children.label: 6 Repealed. , ,Pub. L. 116–260, div. N, title III, § 333(c)134 Stat. 2046Dec. 27, 2020

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: 7 Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There is authorized to be appropriated to the Administration $40,000,000,000 to carry out this subsection.

children.heading: Termination
children.label: 8 Termination
children.text: The authority to carry out grants under this subsection shall terminate on .December 31, 2021

children.heading: Statute of limitations
children.label: 9 Statute of limitations
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of law, any criminal charge or civil enforcement action alleging that a borrower engaged in fraud with respect to the use of an advance received under this subsection shall be filed not later than 10 years after the offense was committed.
